title: ConceptForge Flow
description: A skill pipeline flow definition. Node/edge structure is compatible with Vue Flow (@vue-flow/core) and React Flow (@xyflow/react). Each file in flows/ describes one named path through the skill tree. The skill catalog (hard_gates, user_inputs, folders) lives in shared/contracts/pipeline.json — flows only define the graph and parameters.

id.description: Unique flow identifier, kebab-case. Matches the filename without .json.

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in UI flow picker.

meta.description: Display metadata for the flow picker UI.

category.description: Groups flows in the UI picker.

onboarding.description: Controls the onboarding dialog the orchestrator shows before starting.

input_style.description: freeform = single text box, structured = named fields, repo = repo URL + branch

fields.description: Field IDs shown when input_style is structured.

globals.description: Parameters injected into EVERY skill node in this flow. Per-node data.parameters are merged over globals at runtime (node wins on conflict).

research_depth.description: Passed to the research mode skill and used by orchestrator to decide how much context to gather.

approval_mode.description: checkpoint = CHECKPOINT directives pause for human confirmation. auto_approve = orchestrator continues if quality score passes.

auto_review.description: Run concept-review + lint between phases automatically.

subagent_mode.description: Run each skill node in an isolated subagent context (fresh token budget).

verbosity.description: Controls how verbose skill completion summaries are.

globals.description_extra: additionalProperties:true allows flow authors to define custom globals passed through to all skills.

modes.description: Parallel/utility mode skills that run alongside the main flow. These are not nodes in the graph — they fire automatically based on triggers.

research.description: Research mode: runs concept/discovery/research in parallel with main flow nodes.

triggers.description: Node IDs after which the research skill fires in parallel.

parameters.description: Extra parameters passed to the research skill (merged over globals).

standards.description: Standards discovery mode: runs standards/discover once before implementation.

trigger_after.description: Node ID after which standards/discover runs. Typically 'overview'.

nodes.description: Skill execution nodes + optional group containers. Vue Flow compatible.

edges.description: Directed edges defining execution order. Vue Flow compatible.

entry.description: Node ID of the first skill to execute. Defaults to the node with no incoming edges. If multiple roots exist, this resolves ambiguity.

skill-node.description: A skill execution node. type must be 'skill'.

id.description: Unique within this flow. Typically the short skill name (e.g. 'overview', 'features').

parentNode.description: ID of a group-node that visually contains this node. Position becomes relative to parent.

skill.description: Skill path from skills root, e.g. 'concept/discovery/overview'. Must match an id in pipeline.json steps.

label.description: Display label in flow editor. Defaults to skill's name field.

optional.description: Orchestrator may skip this node based on user preference during onboarding.

parallel_group.description: Nodes with the same parallel_group can execute concurrently once their source edges are satisfied.

subagent.description: Override globals.subagent_mode for this node only.

parameters.description: Skill-specific parameters. Merged over globals at runtime (these win). Passed to the skill as runtime config.

group-node.description: A visual group container (phase label). Does not execute. skill-nodes can use parentNode to nest inside.

style.description: CSS styles for the group container (width, height, background, border, etc.).

flow-edge.description: A directed edge from source node to target node. Vue Flow compatible.

type.description: flow = required sequence edge. optional = target may be skipped. parallel = source and target can overlap execution.

condition.description: Optional condition expression. If false, edge is not traversed.
